title: Fire Hydrant Flow Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool for evaluating fire hydrant flow capacity, system pressure losses, and compliance with NFPA and ISO standards. Supports Lean Six Sigma DMAIC projects and WERC warehouse fire protection audits.

inputs.label: Static Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Measured at hydrant outlet with no flow. Used to determine available system pressure per NFPA 291.

businessContext_i18n.en: Measured at hydrant outlet with no flow. Used to determine available system pressure per NFPA 291.

inputs.label: Residual Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Pressure measured while hydrant is flowing at test rate. Indicates system capacity under demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Pressure measured while hydrant is flowing at test rate. Indicates system capacity under demand.

inputs.label: Test Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Flow rate measured during hydrant flow test using pitot gauge or flow meter.

businessContext_i18n.en: Flow rate measured during hydrant flow test using pitot gauge or flow meter.

inputs.label: Supply Pipe Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Nominal diameter of the main supply pipe feeding the hydrant. Affects friction loss.

businessContext_i18n.en: Nominal diameter of the main supply pipe feeding the hydrant. Affects friction loss.

inputs.label: Supply Pipe Length
inputs.businessContext: Distance from water main to hydrant. Used in friction loss calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Distance from water main to hydrant. Used in friction loss calculations.

inputs.label: Hazen-Williams C Factor
inputs.businessContext: Pipe roughness coefficient. 140 for new steel, 100 for old cast iron. Per ISO 10803 and NFPA 13.

businessContext_i18n.en: Pipe roughness coefficient. 140 for new steel, 100 for old cast iron. Per ISO 10803 and NFPA 13.

inputs.label: Elevation Difference
inputs.businessContext: Height difference between water source and hydrant. Positive if hydrant is above source.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height difference between water source and hydrant. Positive if hydrant is above source.

inputs.label: Number of Hydrants in Test
inputs.businessContext: Number of hydrants flowing simultaneously during test. Affects total demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of hydrants flowing simultaneously during test. Affects total demand.

inputs.label: Hydrant Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of hydrant per NFPA 291. Dry barrel common in cold climates.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of hydrant per NFPA 291. Dry barrel common in cold climates.

inputs.label: Flow Condition
inputs.businessContext: Steady for normal tests, Transient for surge analysis per ISO 4126.

businessContext_i18n.en: Steady for normal tests, Transient for surge analysis per ISO 4126.

inputs.label: NFPA 291 Compliant Test
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if flow test was performed per NFPA 291 standard procedures.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates if flow test was performed per NFPA 291 standard procedures.

rules.message: Static and residual pressures must be greater than zero.
Residual pressure must be less than static pressure.
Test flow rate must be greater than zero.
Pipe diameter must be at least 2 inches.
Hazen-Williams C factor must be between 60 and 150.

thresholds.message: Available flow is below 500 gpm. May not meet minimum fire suppression requirements.
Pressure loss exceeds 20% of static pressure. System may be undersized.
Flow velocity exceeds 20 ft/s. Risk of water hammer and pipe erosion per ISO 4126.
Significant elevation difference detected. Adjust pressure calculations accordingly.
